questions.question: What is inheritance in C++?
keyPoints: Inheritance is a concept in object-oriented programming
Allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class

questions.question: What is polymorphism in C++?
keyPoints: Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms
Can be achieved through function overloading or function overriding

questions.question: What is encapsulation in C++?
keyPoints: Encapsulation is the concept of hiding the implementation details of an object
Can be achieved through access specifiers and abstraction

questions.question: What is abstraction in C++?
keyPoints: Abstraction is the concept of showing only the necessary information to the outside world
Can be achieved through interfaces and abstract classes

questions.question: What is composition in C++?
keyPoints: Composition is the concept of creating an object from other objects
Can be achieved through container classes and composition

questions.question: Explain the concept of inheritance in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Inheritance is a concept in object-oriented programming
Allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class
Example: A child class inheriting the traits of a parent class

questions.question: Explain the concept of polymorphism in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Polymorphism is the ability of an object to take on multiple forms
Can be achieved through function overloading or function overriding
Example: A shape class with different implementations for different shapes

questions.question: Explain the concept of encapsulation in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Encapsulation is the concept of hiding the implementation details of an object
Can be achieved through access specifiers and abstraction
Example: A bank account class with private data members and public member functions

questions.question: Explain the concept of abstraction in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Abstraction is the concept of showing only the necessary information to the outside world
Can be achieved through interfaces and abstract classes
Example: A vehicle class with an abstract interface and concrete implementations
